evals.prompt: Build a todo app using Legend-State where todos persist to localStorage. Each todo has an id, text, and completed status. Show a count of completed todos and include a button to clear all completed.
evals.expected_output: A React component with a Legend-State observable for todos, synced to localStorage via syncObservable or synced with persist config. Uses useValue for reactive counts, For component for rendering list, and a clear completed button.
expectations: Uses Legend-State v3 React consumption patterns such as useValue or Memo instead of deprecated Synced/useSelector patterns
Includes local persistence configuration for the todo state
Renders the todo list with Legend-State-aware array rendering such as For or an equivalent observable-safe pattern
Includes a clear-completed action in the UI

evals.prompt: Create a React dashboard component that displays user stats (name, email, notification count) using Legend-State's fine-grained reactivity so the parent component only renders once. The notification count should update itself without re-rendering the whole dashboard.
evals.expected_output: A React component using Memo or fine-grained reactivity patterns (Memo, useValue) so that individual stat updates don't cause parent re-renders. Uses useObservable for local state.
expectations: Uses fine-grained Legend-State rendering for the notification count such as Memo or an isolated reactive child
Uses modern v3 React consumption patterns instead of deprecated observer plus direct get as the primary pattern
Keeps the parent dashboard component free of direct notification-count reads so notification updates can avoid parent re-render
Shows name, email, and notification count in the output

evals.prompt: Set up a Legend-State observable that syncs user profiles from a REST API using syncedCrud. Include create, update, and delete operations with local persistence fallback.
evals.expected_output: An observable configured with syncedCrud plugin, including get/list, create, update, delete functions, and persist configuration for local fallback.
expectations: Uses the current Legend-State package names and v3 sync APIs
Configures syncedCrud with list or get plus create, update, and delete operations
Includes local persistence configuration for the synced observable
Exports helper functions for creating, updating, deleting, and reading profiles
